label: Africa Corps Personnel in Mali
contestNote: Russia denies official military presence; The Sentry (April 2026) documented 3 major arms convoys in 2025 confirming 2,000–2,500 Africa Corps troops. Previous CSIS/Reuters estimates were ~1,500.
deltaNote: The Sentry's April 2026 investigation revised estimates upward; arms convoys via Guinea routed through Rusal railway and Albayrak Group port infrastructure document a larger force than previously assessed.

label: Civilian Killings — Burkina Faso (Jan 2023–Aug 2025)
contestNote: Traoré junta rejects all HRW findings as politically motivated. Independent access to conflict zones severely restricted.

label: FLA Declared Operations — Mar–Apr 2026
source: FLA statements / TRAC Terrorism / Pravda Mali — April 2026
contestNote: FLA claims are self-reported; FAMa denies or minimizes most incidents. Independent verification from the Kidal theater is not possible.
deltaNote: FLA drone strikes on FAMa bases at Agelhok (Apr 8), Tessalit (Apr 1), and TB2 control center at Amachach in early April 2026

label: WFP Food Aid Suspended — Sahel (Apr 2026)
deltaNote: WFP suspending food assistance to 2M people in Burkina Faso, Mali, Niger, Nigeria, and Chad in April 2026 due to a $620M funding gap. FAO projects 52.8M at acute food insecurity risk in the Jun–Aug 2026 lean season.

label: AES Unified Force — Authorized Strength (Apr 2026)
source: WAVN / Africa Defense Forum / Pravda Burkina Faso — April 2026
contestNote: 15,000 is the authorized strength announced at the April 16–17 CCEMC meeting in Ouagadougou. Actual deployable strength significantly lower; the original December 2025 announcement cited 5,000. Independent verification of field deployment not possible.
deltaNote: From 5,000 at December 2025 inauguration to 15,000 authorized in April 2026 CCEMC meeting. CCEMC declared force 'ready for large-scale operations.' Analyst consensus cautions that G5 Sahel similarly announced ambitious numbers before collapsing.

label: Burkina Faso — Junta Rule Extended To
source: Democracy Now! / The World PRX — April 5–6, 2026
contestNote: Traoré originally promised democratic transition by 2024; extensions granted repeatedly. All political parties banned January 2026. International community rejects the legitimacy of indefinite military rule.
deltaNote: From promised 2024 elections to 2029 extension — all parties banned; Traoré declares democracy 'isn't for us'

label: Mali — April 25 Simultaneous Attack Sites
deltaNote: JNIM and FLA coordinated simultaneous attacks on Kati (Bamako), airport, Sévaré, Gao, and Kidal on April 25, 2026 — described as the largest coordinated offensive since the 2012-2013 war. FLA claims most of Kidal; Defense Minister Sadio Camara's residence destroyed.
